metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_678
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper is the first description of VGLUT2 (formally called DNPI) as a glutamate transporter.

Figure 7c-e shows the stable expression of VGLUT2 in PC12 cells.

Figure 8a presents the [3H]L-glutamate uptake in a fraction of light membranes that include synaptic-like microvesicles prepared by lysing PC12 cells that stably express VGLUT2. The figure shows that these membranes take up more glutamate then the WT control (where no expression of VGLUT2 can be detected). Figure 8c shows specificity of the transporter to glutamate, since the inclusion of non-lablelled glutamate is blocked [3H]L-glutamate uptake. Non-labelled L-aspartate, D-aspartate, glycine, or γ-aminobutyric acid had no effect on [3H]L-glutamate uptake.. Experimental description: To determine whether DNPI transports glutamate into secretory vesicles, we used heterologous expression in rat pheochromocytoma PC12 cells.
